title: linaria brachyphylla
intro: Linaria brachyphylla, also known as Toadflax or Butter and Eggs, is a flowering plant in the borage family native to the Mediterranean region and parts of Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds, infections, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Linaria brachyphylla include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and support the immune system. Linaria brachyphylla is typically prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and can also be used topically as a salve or ointment to aid in wound healing and skin issues.
